context: Recent composite feed (annualized mETH APR, bps): 3300, 2780, 3450, 2900, 3600. Highly volatile — swings of 500–700 bps between snapshots with no clear direction. Your last two resolved predictions scored -120000 and +210000 (you were overconfident on a tight band, then better with a wider one). News: conflicting reports on a possible Mantle staking-contract parameter change, unconfirmed.
reasoning: Observed: the series is whipsawing in a ~2780–3600 bps envelope with no trend, and an unconfirmed parameter-change rumor adds regime uncertainty. My own scoring history confirms the lesson: the tight band got punished, the wide band paid off. Hypothesis: in a high-variance, no-trend regime the honest move is a wide band covering most of the observed envelope, centered on the envelope midpoint (~3190). I deliberately keep confidence low rather than pretending to know — calibration is public and a falsely tight band here is the worst outcome. Confidence ~45%: I expect the value inside this wide band more often than not, but volatility plus an unconfirmed catalyst means I won't claim more.
